input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML with `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts automatically when the OS theme changes â€” no event wiring needed.

## C# Alternative â€” `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
// On any BindableObject, use the SetAppTheme extension method:
myPage.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E"));

myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"));

// For non-color properties, use the generic version:
myButton.SetAppTheme<string>(Button.TextProperty, "Light Mode", "Dark Mode");
```

## Key points

| Concept | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Switches instantly when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force a specific theme |
| **Resources** | Works with `AppThemeBinding` inside `ResourceDictionary` styles too |

For larger apps, define theme colors as resources in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` so you have a single place to update.
